title: zigadenus monoecus
intro: Zigadenus monoecus, also known as death camas, is a highly toxic plant species native to western North America, belonging to the lily family (Liliaceae). The plant's tuberous roots and bulbs contain toxic compounds, including zigadenine and other alkaloids, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. Research suggests that extracts from the plant may have potential health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, but its toxicity limits its safe use. In some herbal preparations, Zigadenus monoecus is used in extremely small, controlled amounts, often in combination with other plants, under the guidance of an experienced practitioner.
